The "heart and estrogen/progestin study" (HERS) was a clinical
trial of hormone therapy for prevention of recurrent heart attacks and
death among 2,763 post-menopausal women with existing coronary heart disease
(CHD) [@HulleyStephen1998RToE].

The trial was conducted at 20 US clinical centers.
Participants were randomized to receive either conjugated equine estrogens
(0.625 mg/day) plus medroxyprogesterone acetate (2.5 mg/day)
or a matching placebo [@HulleyStephen1998RToE].
Women were followed for an average of 4.1 years.

The primary outcome was nonfatal myocardial infarction or CHD death.
